title: Explicit Rule-State Expert For Dry/Wet And Monaco 2025
summary: The FIA rules create hard discontinuities: normal dry races require at least two dry specifications, wet/intermediate usage removes that requirement, and Monaco 2025 had a temporary mandatory two-stop rule.
rationale: The FIA rules create hard discontinuities: normal dry races require at least two dry specifications, wet/intermediate usage removes that requirement, and Monaco 2025 had a temporary mandatory two-stop rule. Those discrete legality constraints change pit incentives in ways that broad tyre-life heuristics can blur.
implementation_hint: Add rule-state features such as `mandatory_stops_remaining_est`, `dry_rule_active`, `wet_rule_exempt`, and `monaco_2025_two_stop_rule`. If Monaco 2025 exists in train/test, either train a tiny expert for that slice or gate a separate score that is blended only there. Also distinguish wet/intermediate races from slick races instead of sharing one decision surface.
expected_effect: Cleaner handling of rare but high-signal slices where the base model is structurally misspecified.
risk: Very sparse slices can create unstable experts; if Monaco 2025 is absent or tiny, keep it as features rather than a standalone model.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
